title: ConvertKit vs Flodesk (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare ConvertKit and Flodesk email marketing platforms. See pricing, features, and ratings to choose the best fit for your needs.
h1: ConvertKit vs Flodesk: Detailed Comparison for 2025
intro: ConvertKit and Flodesk are both popular email marketing platforms, but they serve different creator audiences. ConvertKit emphasizes automation and monetization features, while Flodesk focuses on design-first email creation with flat-rate pricing. This comparison will help you determine which platform best aligns with your business needs and budget.

comparison_table.tool_b: Checkout forms and link in bio

choose_a_if: You want a free plan to start email marketing at no cost
You need advanced automation and tagging for complex workflows
You're selling online courses or digital products and need built-in commerce features
choose_b_if: Design and aesthetics are your top priority for email campaigns
You want predictable flat-rate pricing with unlimited subscribers
You're a visual brand or designer who wants stunning, pre-designed templates

faq.a: Neither is objectively better—it depends on your priorities. ConvertKit excels in automation, e-commerce features, and affordability with a free tier. Flodesk wins on design, customer satisfaction ratings, and straightforward flat-rate pricing. Choose ConvertKit for functionality and Flodesk for visual appeal and simplicity.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, ConvertKit or Flodesk?
faq.a: ConvertKit is cheaper overall because it offers a free tier for up to 1,000 subscribers, making it ideal for beginners. Flodesk starts at $38/month with no free option but provides unlimited subscribers at that price, which becomes cost-effective for larger lists. Your choice depends on subscriber count and budget flexibility.

faq.q: Can I switch from Flodesk to ConvertKit?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support email list exports and imports, making migration possible. You can export your subscribers from Flodesk as a CSV file and import them into ConvertKit. However, you'll need to manually recreate your automations and sequences, as they don't transfer automatically.
